memory.summary: Leisure now feels like a waste when the term project is barely started and due Thursday. I should just crush that quick chore to get it over with.
Still no new info, but my stress is creeping up as the deadline looms. I need to stop wasting energy on this and just execute the task immediately.

desires: Instantly complete the term project to eliminate the looming deadline pressure and stop the stress bleed.
Force myself into a high-intensity work sprint despite my critically low energy to secure the grade immediately.
Discard any social interaction or leisure as a waste of time until the academic task is 100% locked in.

goals.description: Execute a high-intensity work sprint to finish the final 8% of the term project immediately and lock in the 'A' before Thursday.
goals.rationale: The project is at 92.5%, but my impulsive nature demands instant closure to stop the stress bleed. My achievement value (0.5) overrides my low energy and sleep debt; I need that immediate dopamine hit of completion now rather than worrying about tomorrow's fatigue.

goals.description: Assertively block all incoming messages from cohort_3 and ignore any social cues until the submission button is clicked.
goals.rationale: Social interaction is a low-value drain (0.15). To protect the academic win, I must enforce hard boundaries directly. If they try to pull me away, I will cut them off without guilt or explanation.

goals.description: Defer eating, sleeping, and breaks entirely for the next hour to maximize raw output speed.
goals.rationale: My sleep discipline is weak (3), so ignoring basic needs is easy. The short planning horizon means I discount future physical depletion as irrelevant compared to the immediate necessity of securing the grade right now.
